actinic keratosis.description: A rough, scaly patch on the skin caused by long-term exposure to ultraviolet (UV) radiation. It is considered a precancerous skin condition.

acute bronchiolitis.description: A viral infection that causes inflammation of the small airways in the lungs, commonly affecting infants and young children.
precautions: Seek medical care if breathing worsens.

acute bronchitis.description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes, usually caused by a viral infection.
precautions: Consult a doctor if symptoms persist.

acute bronchospasm.description: Sudden tightening of the muscles surrounding the airways, making breathing difficult.

acute kidney injury.description: A sudden decline in kidney function leading to the accumulation of waste products in the body.

acute pancreatitis.description: Sudden inflammation of the pancreas that can range from mild to severe.

acute sinusitis.description: Inflammation of the sinuses lasting less than four weeks, usually caused by a viral infection.

acute stress reaction.description: A temporary psychological response to a highly stressful or traumatic event.
precautions: Consult a mental health professional if symptoms persist.

allergy.description: An exaggerated immune response to normally harmless substances called allergens.

angina.description: Chest pain caused by reduced blood flow to the heart muscle.

anxiety.description: A mental health condition characterized by excessive worry, nervousness, or fear.

appendicitis.description: Inflammation of the appendix that usually requires urgent surgical treatment.

arthritis of the hip.description: Degeneration or inflammation of the hip joint causing pain and stiffness.

asthma.description: A chronic condition in which the airways become inflamed and narrowed, causing breathing difficulties.

benign prostatic hyperplasia (bph).description: Non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland commonly seen in older men.

blepharitis.description: Inflammation of the eyelids, often affecting the area where eyelashes grow.

brachial neuritis.description: Inflammation of the nerves in the shoulder region causing sudden pain and weakness.
precautions: Avoid overuse of the affected arm.

bursitis.description: Inflammation of the fluid-filled sacs that cushion joints.

cholecystitis.description: Inflammation of the gallbladder, often caused by gallstones.

chronic back pain.description: Persistent pain in the back lasting for more than three months.

chronic constipation.description: A digestive condition characterized by infrequent bowel movements or difficulty passing stools for several weeks or longer.

chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (c.description: A chronic lung disease that causes airflow obstruction and breathing difficulties, most commonly due to smoking.

common cold.description: A mild viral infection affecting the nose and throat that usually resolves without specific treatment.
precautions: Cover your mouth while coughing or sneezing.

complex regional pain syndrome.description: A chronic pain condition that usually develops after an injury and causes severe, persistent pain in a limb.
common_symptoms: temperature changes in the affected limb

concussion.description: A mild traumatic brain injury caused by a blow or sudden movement of the head that temporarily affects brain function.

conjunctivitis.description: Inflammation of the conjunctiva, the thin transparent membrane covering the white part of the eye and inner eyelids. It may be caused by infections, allergies, or irritants.
precautions: Avoid touching or rubbing the eyes.
Do not share towels or eye cosmetics.

conjunctivitis due to allergy.description: An allergic inflammation of the conjunctiva triggered by allergens such as pollen, dust, or pet dander.
precautions: Keep windows closed during high pollen seasons.

contact dermatitis.description: A skin condition caused by direct contact with an irritant or allergen, resulting in inflammation and irritation.

cornea infection.description: An infection of the cornea that can threaten vision if not treated promptly. It may be caused by bacteria, viruses, fungi, or parasites.
precautions: Seek immediate medical attention for eye pain.

croup.description: A viral respiratory illness that causes swelling of the upper airway, leading to a characteristic barking cough, primarily affecting young children.
precautions: Use a cool-mist humidifier if recommended.
Seek emergency care if breathing becomes difficult.

cystitis.description: Inflammation of the urinary bladder, most commonly caused by a bacterial urinary tract infection.
precautions: Complete prescribed antibiotics if advised by a doctor.

degenerative disc disease.description: A condition in which the spinal discs gradually wear down over time, leading to pain and reduced flexibility.

dental caries.description: A common dental disease in which tooth enamel is damaged by acids produced by bacteria, leading to cavities.
precautions: Visit the dentist for routine check-ups.

depression.description: A common mental health disorder characterized by persistent sadness, loss of interest, and changes in thinking, behavior, and daily functioning.
precautions: Stay connected with family and friends.

developmental disability.description: A group of conditions that affect physical, learning, language, or behavioral development, usually beginning during childhood.

diverticulitis.description: Diverticulitis is the inflammation or infection of small pouches (diverticula) that develop in the wall of the large intestine. It can cause abdominal pain and digestive problems.
precautions: Eat a high-fiber diet after recovery.

drug reaction.description: A drug reaction is an unwanted response to a medication, ranging from mild skin rashes to severe allergic reactions.
precautions: Stop the medication only after consulting a healthcare provider unless it's an emergency.
Inform healthcare providers about known drug allergies.
Carry allergy information if you have severe reactions.
Seek immediate care for breathing difficulty or facial swelling.

ear drum damage.description: Damage or perforation of the eardrum can affect hearing and may occur due to infection, injury, or sudden pressure changes.
precautions: Avoid inserting objects into the ear.

eczema.description: Eczema, also known as atopic dermatitis, is a chronic skin condition that causes inflammation, itching, and dry skin.

esophagitis.description: Eczema, also known as atopic dermatitis, is a chronic skin condition that causes inflammation, itching, and dry skin.
precautions: Do not lie down immediately after eating.

eustachian tube dysfunction (ear disorde.description: Eustachian tube dysfunction occurs when the tube connecting the middle ear to the back of the nose becomes blocked, causing pressure imbalance and hearing discomfort.
precautions: Avoid sudden pressure changes when possible.
Consult an ENT specialist if symptoms persist.

fungal infection of the hair.description: A fungal infection of the scalp and hair follicles, commonly known as tinea capitis, causing hair loss and scalp irritation.
precautions: Complete the full course of antifungal treatment.

gallstone.description: Gallstones are hardened deposits of digestive fluid that form in the gallbladder and may block the bile ducts.
precautions: Seek medical care for severe abdominal pain.

gastrointestinal hemorrhage.description: Gastrointestinal hemorrhage refers to bleeding anywhere along the digestive tract and can range from mild to life-threatening.
precautions: Follow treatment for underlying digestive conditions.

gout.description: Gout is a form of inflammatory arthritis caused by the buildup of uric acid crystals in the joints.
precautions: Take uric acid-lowering medications if prescribed.

heart attack.description: A heart attack (myocardial infarction) occurs when blood flow to part of the heart muscle is blocked, causing damage to the heart tissue. It is a medical emergency.
common_symptoms: pain radiating to the arm, neck, jaw, or back
precautions: Seek emergency medical care immediately if symptoms occur.
Exercise regularly as advised by a doctor.
Control blood pressure, diabetes, and cholesterol.

heart failure.description: Heart failure is a chronic condition in which the heart cannot pump blood effectively to meet the body's needs.

hemorrhoids.description: Hemorrhoids are swollen veins in the lower rectum or anus that can cause discomfort, bleeding, and itching.
precautions: Avoid prolonged sitting on the toilet.

herniated disk.description: A herniated disk occurs when the soft inner portion of a spinal disk pushes through its outer layer, irritating nearby nerves.

hiatal hernia.description: A hiatal hernia occurs when part of the stomach pushes through the diaphragm into the chest, often leading to acid reflux symptoms.
precautions: Avoid lying down immediately after eating.

hyperemesis gravidarum.description: Hyperemesis gravidarum is a severe form of nausea and vomiting during pregnancy that can lead to dehydration, weight loss, and electrolyte imbalance.
precautions: Stay hydrated with small frequent sips of fluids.
Seek medical care if unable to keep fluids down.

hypertensive heart disease.description: Hypertensive heart disease refers to heart conditions caused by long-standing high blood pressure, which increases the workload on the heart.

hypoglycemia.description: Hypoglycemia is a condition in which blood sugar levels fall below normal, commonly affecting people with diabetes.
precautions: Consume a fast-acting source of glucose.

idiopathic excessive menstruation.description: Idiopathic excessive menstruation is abnormally heavy menstrual bleeding without an identifiable underlying cause.
precautions: Seek medical evaluation for persistent heavy bleeding.

idiopathic irregular menstrual cycle.description: Idiopathic irregular menstrual cycle refers to unpredictable menstrual cycles without a clearly identifiable medical cause.
precautions: Consult a gynecologist if irregularity persists.

idiopathic painful menstruation.description: Idiopathic painful menstruation, also known as primary dysmenorrhea, is menstrual pain that occurs without an underlying pelvic disease. It is caused by uterine muscle contractions during menstruation.
precautions: Apply a heating pad to the lower abdomen.

infectious gastroenteritis.description: Infectious gastroenteritis is an infection of the stomach and intestines caused by viruses, bacteria, or parasites, resulting in diarrhea and vomiting.
precautions: Drink plenty of fluids to prevent dehydration.
Seek medical attention if symptoms are severe.

injury to the arm.description: An injury to the arm may involve muscles, bones, joints, tendons, or ligaments due to trauma or overuse.
precautions: Apply ice during the first 24–48 hours.
Seek medical evaluation for severe pain or deformity.

injury to the leg.description: An injury to the leg may affect muscles, bones, ligaments, tendons, or joints and can range from minor strains to fractures.
precautions: Compress with an elastic bandage if appropriate.
Seek medical care if unable to bear weight.

injury to the trunk.description: An injury to the trunk involves trauma to the chest, abdomen, pelvis, or back and may affect muscles, bones, or internal organs.
precautions: Apply ice for minor soft tissue injuries.
Seek immediate medical attention for severe pain, breathing difficulty, or abdominal tenderness.
Follow medical advice regarding imaging or treatment.

kidney stone.description: Kidney stones are hard deposits of minerals and salts that form inside the kidneys and can cause severe pain when passing through the urinary tract.
precautions: Limit foods high in oxalates if advised.
Seek medical attention for severe pain or fever.

liver disease.description: Liver disease includes conditions that damage the liver and impair its ability to perform essential functions such as detoxification and metabolism.

macular degeneration.description: Macular degeneration is an eye disease that affects the central part of the retina (macula), leading to gradual loss of central vision.
precautions: Eat foods rich in leafy green vegetables.

marijuana abuse.description: Marijuana abuse is the repeated use of cannabis in a way that negatively affects health, daily functioning, or relationships.
precautions: Follow treatment recommendations if diagnosed with substance use disorder.

multiple sclerosis.description: Multiple sclerosis is a chronic autoimmune disease in which the immune system attacks the protective covering of nerve fibers, affecting communication between the brain and body.
precautions: Avoid excessive heat if symptoms worsen.

neurosis.description: Neurosis is a non-psychotic mental health condition characterized by excessive anxiety, emotional distress, and difficulty coping with daily life while maintaining awareness of reality.
precautions: Seek professional mental health support if symptoms persist.

noninfectious gastroenteritis.description: Noninfectious gastroenteritis is inflammation of the stomach and intestines caused by factors other than infections, such as medications, food intolerance, or autoimmune conditions.
precautions: Consult a healthcare provider if symptoms continue.

nose disorder.description: A nose disorder refers to various conditions affecting the nose, including structural abnormalities, inflammation, infections, or chronic irritation.
precautions: Use saline nasal sprays if recommended.
Seek medical evaluation for persistent symptoms.

obstructive sleep apnea (osa).description: Obstructive sleep apnea is a sleep disorder in which breathing repeatedly stops and starts because the airway becomes blocked during sleep.
precautions: Sleep on your side when possible.

otitis externa (swimmer's ear).description: Otitis externa is an infection or inflammation of the outer ear canal, commonly occurring after water becomes trapped inside the ear.
precautions: Avoid inserting cotton swabs or objects into the ear.
Use prescribed ear drops as directed.

otitis media.description: Otitis media is an infection or inflammation of the middle ear, commonly occurring in children but also affecting adults.
precautions: Complete the prescribed course of antibiotics if given.
Manage fever and pain as advised.
Avoid inserting objects into the ear.
Seek medical care if symptoms worsen or hearing decreases.

pain after an operation.description: Pain after an operation is a common postoperative symptom resulting from tissue injury during surgery. It usually improves as healing progresses.
precautions: Avoid strenuous activities until cleared by your surgeon.
Report increasing pain, fever, or wound discharge immediately.

panic disorder.description: Panic disorder is a mental health condition characterized by repeated, unexpected panic attacks accompanied by intense fear and physical symptoms.
precautions: Avoid excessive caffeine and recreational drugs.
Take prescribed medications regularly if advised.

pelvic inflammatory disease.description: Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) is an infection of the female reproductive organs, usually caused by sexually transmitted bacteria.
precautions: Avoid sexual activity until treatment is completed if advised.

peripheral nerve disorder.description: Peripheral nerve disorders affect nerves outside the brain and spinal cord, causing weakness, numbness, or pain, usually in the hands or feet.
precautions: Control underlying medical conditions such as diabetes.

personality disorder.description: A personality disorder is a mental health condition characterized by long-term patterns of thinking, feeling, and behaving that differ significantly from cultural expectations and interfere with daily life and relationships.
precautions: Maintain regular follow-up with a mental health professional.

pneumonia.description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus, making breathing difficult.
precautions: Complete the prescribed antibiotics or antiviral medications.
Seek immediate medical care if breathing becomes difficult.

problem during pregnancy.description: Problems during pregnancy include medical conditions or complications that may affect the health of the mother or the developing baby.
precautions: Seek immediate medical attention for heavy bleeding or severe pain.

psoriasis.description: Psoriasis is a chronic autoimmune skin disease that causes rapid skin cell buildup, resulting in thick, scaly patches.
precautions: Avoid known triggers such as stress and smoking.
Use prescribed topical or systemic medications.

psychotic disorder.description: Psychotic disorders are serious mental health conditions that affect a person's perception of reality, potentially causing hallucinations or delusions.

pyelonephritis.description: Pyelonephritis is a bacterial infection of one or both kidneys. It usually develops when a urinary tract infection spreads to the kidneys.
precautions: Complete the full course of prescribed antibiotics.
Do not delay treatment for urinary symptoms.
Seek immediate medical attention for high fever or severe pain.

pyogenic skin infection.description: A pyogenic skin infection is a bacterial infection of the skin that leads to pus formation. Common examples include boils, abscesses, and cellulitis.
precautions: Keep the affected area clean and dry.

rectal disorder.description: Rectal disorders include conditions affecting the rectum, such as inflammation, fissures, prolapse, or other abnormalities causing pain or bleeding.
precautions: Seek medical evaluation for persistent bleeding.

seasonal allergies (hay fever).description: Seasonal allergies, also called hay fever, are allergic reactions to airborne allergens such as pollen, leading to inflammation of the nose and eyes.
precautions: Avoid outdoor exposure during high pollen counts.
Keep windows closed during allergy season.

sebaceous cyst.description: A sebaceous cyst is a slow-growing, noncancerous lump beneath the skin filled with keratin or oily material. It commonly occurs on the scalp, face, neck, or back.
precautions: Do not squeeze or puncture the cyst.
Seek medical care if the cyst becomes painful or infected.
Follow medical advice if surgical removal is recommended.

sepsis.description: Sepsis is a life-threatening condition that occurs when the body's response to an infection damages its own tissues and organs. It requires immediate medical treatment.
common_symptoms: high fever or low body temperature
precautions: Maintain good hygiene and wound care.

sickle cell crisis.description: A sickle cell crisis is a painful episode in people with sickle cell disease caused by blockage of blood flow due to sickle-shaped red blood cells.
precautions: Seek emergency care for severe pain or breathing difficulty.

sinus bradycardia.description: Sinus bradycardia is a slower-than-normal heart rate originating from the heart's natural pacemaker. It may be normal in healthy individuals or indicate an underlying heart condition.
precautions: Seek medical attention for fainting or chest pain.

skin pigmentation disorder.description: Skin pigmentation disorders are conditions that cause the skin to become lighter, darker, or uneven in color due to changes in melanin production.

spinal stenosis.description: Spinal stenosis is the narrowing of spaces within the spine, putting pressure on the spinal cord or nerves and causing pain or numbness.

spondylosis.description: Spondylosis is an age-related degenerative condition affecting the spine, including the vertebrae and intervertebral discs, which may lead to neck or back pain and stiffness.
precautions: Perform regular stretching and strengthening exercises.
Consult a doctor if pain or numbness worsens.

spontaneous abortion.description: Spontaneous abortion, commonly known as miscarriage, is the natural loss of a pregnancy before the fetus is viable. It requires prompt medical evaluation.
precautions: Avoid strenuous physical activity until evaluated.

sprain or strain.description: A sprain is the stretching or tearing of ligaments, while a strain involves muscles or tendons. Both commonly occur after sudden twisting or overuse.
precautions: Seek medical evaluation if severe pain or inability to move persists.

strep throat.description: Strep throat is a bacterial infection of the throat caused by Group A Streptococcus bacteria, leading to sore throat and fever.
precautions: Complete the full course of prescribed antibiotics.
Stay home until no longer contagious as advised.

stye.description: A stye is a painful, red lump near the edge of the eyelid caused by a bacterial infection of an eyelid gland.
possible_causes: touching the eyes with unclean hands
precautions: Apply warm compresses several times a day.
Avoid wearing contact lenses or eye makeup until healed.

threatened pregnancy.description: Threatened pregnancy refers to vaginal bleeding during early pregnancy while the cervix remains closed and the pregnancy may still continue normally. It requires prompt medical evaluation.
precautions: Avoid strenuous physical activity unless advised otherwise.

urinary tract infection.description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is a bacterial infection affecting any part of the urinary system, most commonly the bladder and urethra.

vaginal cyst.description: A vaginal cyst is a fluid-filled sac that develops on or beneath the vaginal lining. Most are benign and may not require treatment unless they become painful or infected.
precautions: Avoid squeezing or attempting to drain the cyst.
Seek medical evaluation if it enlarges or becomes painful.
Follow prescribed treatment if infection develops.

vaginitis.description: Vaginitis is inflammation of the vagina caused by infections, irritation, or changes in the normal balance of vaginal bacteria or yeast.

vulvodynia.description: Vulvodynia is chronic pain or discomfort of the vulva lasting for at least three months without an identifiable cause.
precautions: Consult a specialist for persistent symptoms.
